content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings:


texttexttext (also called **haystack**)
patternpatternpattern (also called **needle**)
Your task is to determine the **index of the first occurrence** of patternpatternpattern in texttexttext.

If patternpatternpattern does not appear in texttexttext, return **-1**.

Function DeclarationFunction NameSearchSearchSearch – This function finds the **index of the first occurrence** of a substring within a given string.

Parameters
texttexttext : The main string (haystack) in which the pattern is searched.
patternpatternpattern : The substring (needle) to search for.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the **0-based index** of the first occurrence of patternpatternpattern in texttexttext.
Returns **-1** if the pattern does not exist in the text.
Constraints
1≤∣text∣,∣pattern∣≤1051 \leq |text|, |pattern| \leq 10^51≤∣text∣,∣pattern∣≤105
Both strings consist of **lowercase English letters** (`'a'`–`'z'`) only

Input Format

The first line contains a string texttexttext.
The second line contains a string patternpatternpattern.


Output Format

Print a single integer — the index of the first occurrence of patternpatternpattern in texttexttext.
If the pattern is not present, print **-1**.
constraints: Both strings consist of lowercase English letters ('a'–'z') only
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a string text (also called haystack). The second line contains a string pattern (also called needle).
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer — the index of the first occurrence of pattern in text. If the pattern is not present, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The substring 'ming' first appears in 'programmingisfun' starting at index 7.
The substring 'tree' is not present in 'datastructures', so the output is -1.
The substring 'll' first appears in 'hello' starting at index 2.

solution.approach: The KMP algorithm is used to find the first occurrence of a substring in a string. It works by precomputing a lookup table that stores the longest proper prefix which is also a suffix for each substring of the pattern. This table is used to skip characters in the text that cannot match the pattern, thus reducing the number of comparisons needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n + m) per test case, where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern. The precomputation of the lookup table takes O(m) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(m) — the dominant structure is the lookup table of size m.
